programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Historian Tim Marlow explores the life and work of 17th-century visionary Dutch artist Rembrandt Harmenszoon van Rijn, who was famed for his skilful handling of light and shade in paintings portraying the human form. Born in Leiden in 1606, the eighth of nine children, his passion for art led him to an apprenticeship with a local painter, and from the time he was a 19-year-old he had a studio in Amsterdam, where he produced 75 self-portraits. Demand for his talents soon grew and success came when he was commissioned to paint The Anatomy Lesson of Dr Tulp.(n)

Romantic comedy, starring Richard Gere and Julia Roberts, with Ralph Bellamy and Laura San Giacomo.(n)

programme.title: Baby's Day Out (1994)
programme.desc: Three bungling kidnappers hatch a plan to pose as society reporters and snatch the baby son of a wealthy couple. However, the infant escapes on to the streets of New York, evading the crooks' every attempt to recapture him. Comedy, starring Joe Mantegna, Lara Flynn Boyle, Joe Pantoliano and Brian Haley.(n)

programme.title: The Last Airbender (2010)
programme.desc: A chosen one with mastery of the four elements is charged with keeping the peace between four nations. When he disappears while still a boy, one of these factions sees a chance to gain absolute power and wages war on the others. After 100 years, the chosen one is found and must find a way to restore harmony. Fantasy adventure based on the cartoon series, starring Noah Ringer and Dev Patel.(n)

programme.title: Flushed Away (2006)
programme.desc: A pampered pet rodent is flushed down a toilet and ends up in a vast underground rat city in the sewers of London. He is befriended by a quick-witted rat who offers to help him find his way home, but first the hapless pair have to escape the clutches of an evil toad bent on ridding the rodent metropolis of its residents. Animated comedy, with the voices of Hugh Jackman, Kate Winslet, Ian McKellen, Shane Richie, Jean Reno and Bill Nighy.(n)

programme.title: The Adventures of Tintin (2011)
programme.desc: A reporter's purchase of a model ship brings him under threat from a ruthless criminal, as it is one of a series of clues to the location of the real vessel, which holds a pirate's lost treasure. He sets off on a globetrotting quest to find it, assisted by a drunken sea captain, two bumbling detectives and his faithful dog. Steven Spielberg's animated adventure based on Herge's comic-strip series, with the voices of Jamie Bell, Andy Serkis and Daniel Craig.(n)

programme.title: Knight and Day (2010)
programme.desc: A woman has a chance encounter with a rogue secret agent, who has been framed as a traitor and is on the run from his own superiors. Realising she has been mistaken for his accomplice and is in danger for knowing too much, the fugitive kidnaps her and drags her on a dangerous journey around the world, as he sets out to clear his name and protect a vitally important invention. Comedy adventure, with Tom Cruise, Cameron Diaz and Viola Davis.(n)

programme.title: Independence Day: Resurgence (2016)
programme.desc: Two decades have passed since Earth was saved from an extraterrestrial invasion, and in the years since, the aliens' salvaged technology has been used to transform the world. However, it may not be enough to save the human race when a second, even deadlier attack begins. Sci-fi adventure, starring Jeff Goldblum, Liam Hemsworth and Bill Pullman.(n)

programme.title: Punisher: War Zone (2008)
programme.desc: The ruthless vigilante considers hanging up his guns when he learns a gangster he killed was actually an undercover FBI agent. However, he must return to his war on crime when a mobster who was hideously disfigured at his hands assembles a gang of violent criminals to cause mayhem on the streets of New York. Action thriller based on the Marvel comic-book, starring Ray Stevenson and Dominic West.(n)

programme.title: Assault on Precinct 13 (1976)
programme.desc: A virtually deserted police station in downtown Los Angeles is besieged by heavily armed violent street thugs. The cops realise they cannot fight off the overwhelming forces alone, so enter into an uneasy alliance with the criminals they have locked in the cells. John Carpenter's thriller, starring Darwin Joston, Austin Stoker and Laurie Zimmer.(n)

programme.title: Garfield 2: A Tale of Two Kitties (2006)
programme.desc: The lazy moggy stows away in his owner's luggage and ends up in London. He is promptly mistaken for an English upper-class cat, who has recently inherited a grand estate, and moves into his stately home - much to the dismay of the real aristocratic feline's conniving relation, who is bent on swindling him out of his money. Family comedy, starring Breckin Meyer, Billy Connolly and Jennifer Love Hewitt, with the voices of Bill Murray and Tim Curry.(n)

programme.title: Kubo and the Two Strings (2016)
programme.desc: A boy unwittingly summons an evil spirit to wreak havoc on his peaceful village home. The only hope of defeating the vengeful creature is to find a magical suit of armour belonging to the boy's father - but finding it will entail matching wits with an assortment of monstrous foes. Animated adventure, with the voices of Charlize Theron, Art Parkinson and Matthew McConaughey.(n)

programme.title: Local Hero (1983)
programme.desc: An American oil company representative is sent to buy a Scottish coastal village so it can be demolished to make way for a new refinery. He gradually takes a shine to the place, while the locals are more interested in the money they can get for parting with it. Bill Forsyth's comedy drama, starring Peter Riegert, Burt Lancaster, Denis Lawson, Peter Capaldi, Jenny Seagrove and Fulton Mackay.(n)

programme.title: Paddington (2014)
programme.desc: An earthquake forces a bear from the Peruvian jungle to start a new life in London. He finds modern Britain to be very different from the stories told to him by his aunt, but makes a new home with a kindly family. However, a sinister taxidermist intends to turn him into a museum exhibit. Comedy based on Michael Bond's series of children's books, starring Hugh Bonneville, Sally Hawkins and Nicole Kidman, with the voice of Ben Whishaw.(n)

programme.title: Eddie the Eagle (2016)
programme.desc: Biopic of British sporting underdog Eddie Edwards. Determined to fulfil his dreams of becoming an Olympic athlete, Edwards trains as a ski jumper - a field in which Britain had no other competitors. He secures a place in the 1988 Winter Olympics, where his can-do attitude makes him a media sensation in the face of a losing streak. Starring Taron Egerton, Hugh Jackman and Christopher Walken.(n)

In the first edition, Aina, a music producer from London, wants to find someone adventurous who she can have a deeper connection with, while Guildford-based designer Mal - who has dated men and women - is curious to see who she will be drawn to when all options are laid bare before her.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp creates some quick and easy 1960s-style pop art with a spray can to help transform Amber and Colin's living room.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi helps a Stockport couple with a budget of Â£113,000 search for a holiday apartment in their favourite town of Mijas in Spain. Joanne and Andy Green have spent their vacations there for more than a decade.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi shows Essex-born Jackie and her Portuguese partner Hilario a selection of properties that fall within their Â£62,000 budget in the Eastern Algarve, where the couple want to buy their dream holiday home.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit a landed gent in Gloucestershire, converting a gilded picture frame into a flat-screen TV surround and hoping to sell a Fordson tractor.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien realise profit potential in an old crop sprayer, a cast-iron fire guard and some old wooden workshop clamps.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: It's breaking point for Tim and Rebecca as they struggle to complete their kitchen as an expensive mishap with granite worktops put Rebecca's dream room in jeopardy. Meanwhile, at Ben and Billie's chateau, the duo welcome 60 guests for the wedding of Billie's brother Pete.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the King's Head in Addlethorpe, Lincolnshire, where landlady Helen Coy and her bar manager and best friend Emma Green are pulling out all the stops to win. The B&B owners are taken back to their childhood at the funfair, At dinner, B&B beginners Louise and Andy Macbeth rattle nerves when Louise reveals her past career as a hospitality journalist - leaving pub veteran Helen less than impressed.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: B&B newcomers Andy and Louise Macbeth invite their rival hoteliers to spend the night at the dog-friendly Beck Hall in North Yorkshire, where an extra-critical Helen and Emma discover what they believe to be stray dog hairs in their bed. A fishing trip makes Terri feel a little out of sorts, and when the time arrives for feedback, the managers are surprised to learn two of their guests have indicated they would not come back for a second stay.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Albaston in Torquay for the third day of the competition, where they are welcomed by owners and avid souvenir collectors, Kevin and Julie Shadbolt. Terri and Stuart are impressed by their room, but are startled by the memorabilia in the communal lounge, and expectations are raised at dinner when Kevin reveals he has a secret recipe for success when it comes to breakfast. However, at feedback the next day, tensions begin to rise.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay of the week is in Bournemouth, at Terri and Stewart's specialist dance holiday venue the Hotel Piccadilly, where a class in the grand ballroom soon has the guests in a spin. However, temperatures rise over dinner when the simmering tension between Louise and Helen finally boils over.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners meet up for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. King's Head landlady Helen challenges Louise and Andy about the problems they reported on cleanliness in the bathroom. while Louise and Andy from Beck Hall get hot under the collar when the others criticise their dog-friendly approach.(n)

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: The British owners of a Spanish seafood restaurant try to lost recover revenue after a blocked drain impacted the business in a detrimental way. Plus, after a quiet summer, hopes remain that a final flurry of guests will offer a welcome boost to a fledging French B&B.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw plan a surprise restoration of a 1972 Peugeot 504 cabriolet in need of a major overhaul - but nearly get caught smuggling it back to their workshop.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie prepares to end the year in style by throwing a Christmas dinner to remember. However, with the ancient range cooker out of action and the 19th-century bread oven put into service cooking the turkey, it's a tall order to deliver a five-course extravaganza for 17 guests. Angel sets Dick the task of finding and chopping the perfect logs for her Christmas table display in their 12-acre grounds. Christmas cheer threatens to run dry for south Londoners Lee and Belinda as freezing temperatures and a broken boiler put their first ever festive event in peril.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Following the work of Ed and Rowena Waghorn as they continue to build a handcrafted, five-bedroom house on an eight acre smallholding in Herefordshire, 10 years in the making. Progress on the build was slow as Ed decided to do most of the work himself, and after four years the house was still a shell. In 2012 Kevin McCloud decided to follow the build for another five years. In the end, it's just possible this house might have turned out to be a masterpiece.(n)

programme.title: Thou Shalt Not Kill
programme.desc: Despite his suspension, Giorgio Lombardi continues to investigate the anomalies surrounding Lucia's murder. Meanwhile, Iacopo Nardi takes Simone hostage and Valeria rouses herself while fighting her dependence on drugs to help with the investigation into Matteo's death. When Simone is found, he is too traumatised to speak, and Andrea and Valeria must pick their way through a maze of false trails and manipulation of evidence to find the sad truth. In Italian.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Eighty-four-year-old Paula is referred to St George's for urgent treatment, after a routine blood test detected that she has a dangerously high level of potassium in her blood. Meanwhile, 74-year-old Derek is brought into A&E after suffering a suspected stroke. As he goes for emergency surgery, his devoted wife Mary is determined to help him recover. Plus, 31-year-old fireman Glenn arrives with a suspected abscess in his throat; he is joined by his twin brother Dean, and the pair prove to be inseparable.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Keen young rugby player Robert has a dangerously high heart rate and may need to have a pacemaker fitted - a scary thought for the nine-year-old's father. Mark, 32, has fallen 30ft from scaffolding while doing some DIY, and there's concern he may have damaged his spine. Christine, 74, arrives in A&E after an accident in her garden, and she's suffered nasty injuries to her face.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Bishop Brennan orders Ted and Dougal to campaign against a controversial film - but as a result of their protest it becomes the biggest box-office hit in Craggy Island's history. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon, Frank Kelly and Pauline McLynn.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: TV personality Henry Sellers arrives to judge an all-priest version of Stars in Their Eyes, prompting intense rivalry between the star-struck Craggy Island clerics and their Rugged Island counterparts. Comedy, with Dermot Morgan.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi helps sisters Annabel and Victoria, who have a budget of Â£100,000, to find an accessible apartment on the Costa del Sol, where their parents once owned a holiday home.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi helps Irene Evans search for a home in western Andalusia, Spain, within commuting distance of Gibraltar, where she hopes to find work as a paralegal. She has decided to leave the UK after a tumultuous few years and has a budget of Â£90,000.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with cautious first-time buyers Mark and Ellie Devine, who relocated from London to Bristol, and Jo and Pete in Archway, north London. With Jo's diagnosis of multiple sclerosis, easy access and a close proximity to transport links were vital considerations for Phil's original search brief.(n)

programme.title: Phil Spencer's Stately Homes
programme.desc: The property expert visits Hopetoun House in the Scottish Lowlands, and investigates the huge amount of time, money and resources that led to its creation. Having made a fortune from lead mining, the Hopetoun family spent big on its country pile, employing not one, but two celebrated Scottish architects. Phil inspects the lavish rooms, many of which are unchanged since Georgian times and meets the staff and tours the 6500-acre estate.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dairy farmer's wife and Disney enthusiast Charlene Fry-Henderson hosts the first dinner party in Dumfries and Galloway, where she hopes that her locally reared meat, home cooking, and entertainment involving a plastic cow will secure her the Â£1,000 prize. Her guests are local radio presenter Tommy Jardine, banker Olive Melville, prison custody officer Tammy Rice and trainee teacher Josh Jones.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Horse enthusiast and banker Olive Melville hosts the second dinner party in Dumfries and Galloway, where she hopes to impress her guests with a crab starter and mousse dessert - but things take a turn for the worse when a conversation about the royal family divides the diners.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Local radio DJ Tommy Jardine hosts the third dinner party in Dumfries and Galloway, and prepares chicken stuffed with haggis - but first he must continue a family tradition and rustle up a sheep for his entertainment. Tommy then tries to impress his guests with a touch of celebrity name-dropping, but young dance DJ and trainee teacher Josh trumps him at his own game.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

He plans lamb pasanda and tandoori monkfish, but Tammy Rice finds something in her glass that wasn't on the menu. However, Josh hopes his disco entertainment might impress the guests enough to bag him the Â£1,000 prize.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Tammy Rice hosts the final evening in Dumfries and Galloway, hoping to win the Â£1,000 prize with her no-nonsense approach and traditional Scottish fare. The dinner-party novice rustles up chorizo and cous cous stuffed peppers for her starter and incorporates haggis into her chicken-based main course.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Suzy Way, the owner of a modern and unconventional B&B near Loughborough, Leicestershire, and her sister Niki Brown prepare an activity-packed day featuring water sport wakeboarding and a home-made curry to win their guests over.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.desc: Becky, 59, is rushed in after a suspected stroke, and medics are concerned she may have had a bleed on the brain. She's sent for an urgent CT scan with possible spinal injuries. Meanwhile, 88-year-old Edith is kept under observation after collapsing at home.(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Over the last decade, incidents of violence perpetrated by young men have risen by 22 per cent and this edition examines the stories behind some of these calls and crimes. In Swindon, officers Emily Grigor and Simon Sanghera are called to arrest a man in his early twenties who has ripped off his top in a drunken rage before putting his fist through a wall and his foot through a window in a bar.(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: The return of the programme following the emergency services, this time focusing on the work of the police, paramedics and fire service in Wiltshire. The first edition examines the rise in racially aggravated hate crimes, with the mother of a nine-year-old girl calling 999 to report that her daughter has been racially abused by two 11-year-olds while out playing. PC James Isaac arrests a 29-year-old man who targeted a 17-year-old white girl with text messages after coming across photos of her and her black boyfriend on Facebook.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cora, 62, arrives by air ambulance following a high-speed road accident, which left her trapped in the car for more than an hour. Her husband Rae, 65, who was driving, is brought in soon after, and staff are concerned he may have sustained serious head, neck and internal injuries. Scott, 23, has badly cut his knee after a night out, and while he's being treated, he reflects on his schizophrenia diagnosis as a teenager. Retired carpenter Richard, 68, has fallen and hit his head on the pavement, but medics also want to look into his unusually low blood pressure.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May visit Africa on a mission to find the definitive source of the Nile, the world's longest river, which spans more than 4,000 miles. programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are sent to Bolivia, where they are challenged to travel through the rainforest and journey to the coast of Chile, driving second-hand off-road vehicles bought locally for a maximum of £3,500 each. Along the way, they encounter drug lords and feel the effects of altitude sickness as they negotiate the region's roads, one of which is a notoriously dangerous 40-mile mountain pass dubbed El Camino de la Muerte - the Death Road.(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: The tensions of sharing a weekend cottage with friends are much in evidence as Lee and Lucy spend three nights in the New Forest with Toby and Anna. The beautiful countryside is forgotten as they squabble over who has the better bedroom, where they should go on their day trips, who should do the cooking and who should stay behind on the last day to tidy up the cottage. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton, Hugh Dennis and Abigail Cruttenden.(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee thinks the twins are too young to be taught about sex at school and that their teacher Miss Anstis is too stuck up to teach it. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates the documentary exploring Earth's natural history and the factors that have affected it. The first edition examines how the sun affects life on Earth through the seasons, following a polar bear preparing its cubs for a long journey to avoid becoming stranded on floating glaciers. Narrated by David Attenborough.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: A tour of the mightiest rivers in the world, from the deep waters of Siberia's Lake Baikal, home to the world's only freshwater seal, to the wetlands downstream of Earth's tallest waterfall patrolled by frenzied piranhas. There is also footage of the lightning-quick ambush of wildebeest by crocodiles in Africa and macaque monkeys swimming underwater to catch crabs in Indonesia. Presented by David Attenborough.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the world's caves, including the Lechuguilla Cave in America, which contains chambers filled with 6m-long crystals. He observes creatures in Texas and Thailand that never see daylight, and inside Deer Cave in Borneo he finds five million bats whose droppings support an entire ecosystem.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: The nature series looks at animals that live on some of the world's highest mountains. Cameras show gelada baboons screaming to frighten off predators in Ethiopia, demoiselle cranes struggling to migrate over the Himalayas, snow leopards hunting on the peaks of Pakistan and a wild giant panda nursing a baby in a hillside cave. Presented by David Attenborough.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough focuses on animals living in deserts. Cameras explore the dry Atacama of Chile, where guanacos survive by licking dew off cacti, and capture the rare sight of wild camels eating snow to stay hydrated on Mongolia's Gobi plains. In the rocky Namibian desert, elephants desperately search for food and lions wander the wilderness hunting oryx.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Camera crews follow the activities of wildlife in the Arctic and Antarctic, including an emperor penguin colony's nine-month migration across sea ice to its breeding ground. The team also takes aerial and underwater shots to capture humpback whales feeding and tracks a male polar bear as it swims 100km to find new ice from which to hunt seals.(n)

programme.title: Graham Hill: Driven
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of motor racing star Graham Hill, who became the only man to win the Formula One World Championship, Indianapolis 500 and Le Mans 24-hour race - but whose reckless streak was to prove his undoing. Among those sharing their memories of the charismatic Englishman are his son Damon - who became Formula One world champion himself in 1996 - and Jackie Stewart.(n)

programme.title: Jim Clark: The Quiet Champion
programme.desc: Profile of the two-time Formula One champion, who was killed at the height of his career. Featuring rare archive footage from family home-movies, candid interviews with the driver himself and comments by close friends, including Jackie Stewart, who reminisces about their time spent together racing when they were known as Batman and Robin.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: The wildlife on the great plains of Africa, Asia, North America and the Arctic, which cover a quarter of the land on Earth. The distinctive Tibetan fox is captured on film for the first time, while a herd of caribou migrates 2,000 miles across the tundra in search of grass. Night vision equipment tracks a pride of 30 lions hunting elephants in the tropical African savannah near one of the region's few remaining waterholes. Presented by David Attenborough.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: An insight into woodlands around the world, from the stunted conifers cloaked in snow and ice in the Arctic forests, to the broadleaf woods in North America and Europe. Also featured are the 100m-high giant redwoods of California, as well as Madagascar's unusual upside-down trees, which store water in their swollen trunks and harbour strange wildlife. Presented by David Attenborough.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough observes some of the varied species to be found in tropical rainforests, from the amusing courtship rituals of the birds of paradise and a chimpanzee group defending its territory to a flying-frog mating orgy and the sinister parasitic fungi cordyceps, which eat their victims alive.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Following the epic journey of a humpback whale and its calf as they travel from a tropical paradise to the species' great feeding ground in the icy polar seas. David Attenborough also observes footage of colonies of seabirds in the Arabian desert and surfing dolphins in Australia that swim onto the beach to catch their fish.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: An exploration of the survival techniques used by creatures inhabiting the deep oceans of the world, where adaptation is paramount for those dwelling on the surface as well as below. Pods of dolphins are filmed charging in search of prey, surprising methods for conserving energy are revealed as deep-sea octopuses 'fly' with wings, and vampire squid use bioluminescence to create a colourful display.(n)

programme.desc: Status Quo play Pictures of Matchstick Men on Top of the Pops in 1968, the Who perform Long Live Rock in the Old Grey Whistle Test studio, Dire Straits deliver Tunnel of Love and Lynyrd Skynyrd bring a taste of the American South with Sweet Home Alabama. programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage from BBC music shows of the 1970s. Including electric performances by the Rolling Stones, Eric Clapton, David Bowie, Motorhead, Nazareth, AC/DC and George Thorogood, as well as acoustic material by Michael Chapman, Horslips and Paco de Lucia.(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Broadcaster Chris Tarrant continues his travels along the world's most challenging railways. Here he attempts to cross the Andes, setting off from the coast of Chile and heading for the Bolivian capital of Sucre. Along the way he traverses some of the driest and most inhospitable environments on the planet, hitches rides on unusual trains and deals with altitude sickness at more than 3,000 metres.(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Broadcaster Chris Tarrant continues his travels along the world's most challenging railways. programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Chris Tarrant on the Polar Express
programme.desc: Chris sets out to reach the northernmost railway station on Earth, inside Russia's Arctic Circle. This is an epic 2,000-mile adventure from Moscow into Arctic Siberia, and begins with a marathon 19-hour leg. Along the way he enjoys a reindeer stew with a local family and takes the controls of one of the massive locomotives that power the trains, before pressing on along railways built under the brutal regime of Joseph Stalin.(n)

programme.title: Operation Gold Rush with Dan Snow
programme.desc: The historian follows in the footsteps of 19th-century gold prospectors, attempting to find riches in a remote region on the edge of the Canadian arctic. He is joined on his expedition by polar explorer Felicity Aston and physiologist Dr Kevin Fong. The first challenge of the trek is to cross Alaska's Coast Mountains, where they retrace two routes set by 19th-century miners and brave icy rivers, avalanche-prone slopes and near vertical ascents.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Predators and prey in the open oceans come under the scrutiny of David Attenborough. Marlin track down a shoal of sardines as a sei whale scoops up the leftovers, dolphins herd mackerel and attract the attention of a sailfish with the resonance of their sonar, and giant sunfish queue up to allow other species, including seagulls, to feed on their parasites.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Marine life at the poles, where winter temperatures frequently fall to a numbing -40C. Even when the sun reappears in the spring, its weak rays take a long time to melt the ice. David Attenborough describes how wildlife including seals, whales, polar bears and penguins overcome the elements to survive through prolonged cold spells.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: An exploration of the world's most densely inhabited waters, where variations in temperature owing to the year-long cycle of the sun encourage a vast array of marine life, from seals and sharks in the winter, to lobster and salmon in the summer, dolphins in the autumn and the walking handfish in spring. Narrated by David Attenborough.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Veteran naturalist David Attenborough explores the fragile marine ecosystem of the coral reef, where all creatures, including the corals themselves, have to fight for survival. From tiny beginnings, the reef grows and develops over hundreds of years, providing support for innumerable animals - yet one storm can threaten the entire community.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough investigates the opportunities tides create for marine life, showing giant stingrays gliding on currents for a rest, South African sea snails surfing the waves in pursuit of a meal, crabs playing football on a beach, and bottlenose dolphins embarking on a dangerous game of catch. In the oceanic world each turn of the tide can mean life or death for its inhabitants.(n)

However, Gav's cabin crew training seems to be missing when he doesn'tgive his guests any cutlery and reveals his alter ego. Next, Paul attempts to inject some culture into the proceedings by serving Wagyu beef fillets but is shocked when Gav demands his very well done. Drama teacher Kelly hosts a fairy-tale-themed dinner party, while on the final night American teacher Sarah-Ann tries to broaden her guests' palates with a menu of old family favourites.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Musician Gerry and his manager Kathy live in Warwickshire, but yearn for the sea and they cannot wait to buy a holiday home on the coast. Kathy loves the wild shores of Pembrokeshire in west Wales, while Gerry is inspired by the crystal waters of the Greek island of Zakynthos. Going head to head to find them their dream property is Jasmine Harman in the UK and Jonnie Irwin in Greece. Each has a £200,000 budget to play with and three amazing options to line up.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Egypt's Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: Archaeologists hunt for Queen Cleopatra's lost tomb and dive the Mediterranean to explore the sunken ruins of ancient Alexandria. Another team take a batch of 4,000-year-old mummies to the hospital to scan for hidden treasure and a lawyer-turned-archaeologist discovers a secret tunnel network at a forgotten temple that could lead her to the jackpot - the tomb of the last Pharaoh of Egypt.(n)

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales explore the Norfolk Broads. This is the first time the couple have visited the area, and they learn about the unique way of life that has developed over the centuries in this network of slow-moving rivers, fens, marshes and waterlogged woodlands. Their trip takes them to a marshman's cottage, the ruins of St Benet's Abbey; and a local Norfolk wherry, and brings to mind events from the book Swallows and Amazons.(n)

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Dressed head-to-toe in pink, Gibby, the family's former au pair, makes a visit - surprising the boys who have absolutely no memory of her. The visitor soon gets on Mum and Dad's nerves, as not only does she cry at just about anything, but is also obsessed with traditional English cream tea after becoming hooked on the stuff when she stayed with the Goodmans. To make matters even more fraught, Jim pops round and instantly develops an infatuation with her. Comedy, starring Simon Bird, Tom Rosenthal, Tamsin Greig, Paul Ritter, Mark Heap and Sally Phillips.(n)

Plus, fast fashion's filthiest secrets are unwrapped.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Fremont, California, in an attempt to save a restaurant that is tearing the owners' marriage apart. When the business started to fail, the couple decided to splash out on refurbishments to recover their glory days, but their efforts have been futile and now their relationship is at stake.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Baltimore, Maryland, where Denise Whiting has alienated customers by trademarking the word 'hon', a term of endearment used by the local community. programme.title: The Mum Who Got Tourette's
programme.desc: Elizabeth is a typical suburban housewife and mother of three with one big difference - without warning, shortly after her 40th birthday, she developed Tourette's syndrome - a neurological condition that causes involuntary tics, some of them offensive. This one-off documentary follows Elizabeth and her family over the course of a busy summer, including a holiday in Cornwall, GCSE results and meeting her son's new girlfriend - experiences amplified by the unexpected impact of Elizabeth's condition.(n)

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Carol Vorderman, Kelly Brook, Rob Rinder and Mo Gilligan take part in the final charity culinary challenge, overseen by judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith, and presenters Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig, making her final appearance in the tent before being replaced by Matt Lucas for the next series. The contestants must take on three tricky challenges - a sandwich biscuit for the signature round, a sweet bread-based technical, and a cake showstopper which reveals their favourite guilty pleasure.(n)

programme.title: Coronavirus: How Clean Is Your House?
programme.desc: With the bulk of the UK population staying in their homes for fear of getting infected by the coronavirus, this documentary looks at just how safe it is behind closed doors. Two families have their houses forensically swept for fingerprints to find evidence of what they may be unwittingly spreading around. There is also a look at how Covid-19 uses people's behaviour to get inside in the first place and a round-up of the best products to stop the virus in its tracks - and which ones will do no good.(n)

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Collie cross puppy Joey is a popular dog around the kennels, and has been a reliable buddy for the anxious dogs - but will he prove the same support to Holly, Adam and their son Oscar? Toy poodle Gizmo tries to overcome his social anxieties to win the hearts of Fiona and Maisie, a mother and daughter coming to terms with their future separation, and a lurcher with a broken tail requires lots of care - and with any luck, a forever home.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three previous restaurants he tried to save, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he heads for West Babylon, New York, where he helped the inexperienced owners of restaurant Classic American deal with their escalating debts, before returning to Davide in Boston, an Italian restaurant run by two warring brothers. Finally, he visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island, run by hot-headed owner Abby.(n)

programme.title: We'll Meet Again (1943)
programme.desc: A young dancer trying to launch her career during the Second World War discovers a talent for singing. As she becomes a huge star, she encounters a snobbish classical composer who rejects popular music as vulgar. She strives to change his mind and see the perfect musical team they could be. Musical, starring Vera Lynn and Geraldo.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Sam's Mediterranean Kabob Room in Monrovia, California, and within minutes of his arrival, is caught in the crossfire of an argument between the owner, his wife and their children, all of whom are feeling the pressure of working seven days a week. Can the chef save the business and mend the warring family's frayed relationships?(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to the coastal community of Long Beach, California, to save an Italian restaurant that was opened in 1958 and now under the misguided leadership of Nino, who blamed by brother Michael and sister Carina for the eatery's failings. Their 78-year-old mother Inge desperate for Nino to turn the business around, but the feuding family members will have to solve their differences first.(n)

However, his servers and diners tell a different story, and his resistance to change leads to some explosive confrontations.(n)

Plus, fast fashion's filthiest secrets are unwrapped.(n)

programme.title: House
programme.desc: The cantankerous medic receives a visit at work from his therapist Dr Nolan. Medical drama starring Hugh Laurie, with Andre Braugher (Homicide: Life on the Street).(n)

programme.desc: Catching up with tenants and landlords from previous episodes, including a man living in a damp and mouldy flat, who was unable to sleep in the bedroom due to the horrendous conditions. The programme also revisits a 70-year-old landlady whose tenants stopped paying the rent for a year, and the council's Rogue Landlord Team in Lewisham, who learned where a slum property owner's reputation for a short fuse came from.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Matt and Garry are in Wolverhampton, where a woman who owes Â£2,000 in parking fines denies knowing the debtor - despite being a relative. But when her mother also gets involved, tempers start to fray. Stewart and Iain confront a woman in Cheshire who is refusing to pay fees to a veterinary practice, claiming her dog has been misdiagnosed, while Paul and Max collect Â£5,000 owed by a banned company director in east London accused of mis-selling assets on the stock market.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

Stewart and Elmor deal with a sensitive case in Liverpool, as they repossess a property where the tenant has failed to pay any rent for almost a year.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court Enforcement agents evict a large group of squatters barricaded into a disused nursery in London, and have to resort to smashing their way in as they refuse to leave. Meanwhile, agents in Bristol try to recover over Â£10,000 owed by a plumbing business to a supplier, and emotions run high when they attempt to evict a tenant who pleads her innocence in West London.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: An episode combining new and unforgettable past cases. The High Court Enforcement Agents head to Manchester to track down over Â£5,000-worth of debt, and seize an expensive car in the process. Later in south London, the agents deal with a violent debtor who threatens them with a concealed weapon, and in Liverpool, they are met with lies and aggression from a mechanic who owes nearly Â£3,000.(n)

programme.desc: Alan and Charlie take Jake shopping when he prepares to start a new school - but soon land themselves in trouble. Starring Charlie Sheen, Jon Cryer and Angus T Jones.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.sub-title: Putting Swim Fins on a Cat

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Jake disobeys his father's orders and sneaks out of the house to attend a concert. Meanwhile, Charlie's girlfriend is embarrassed to be seen with him at an important dinner after a medication mix-up makes him change character. Charlie Sheen and Jon Cryer star.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Stewart and Vic take on an eviction case in Nottingham, where a landlord wants his property back after months of rent arrears and alleged anti-social behaviour - and find themselves confronted by a volatile tenant who insists he has the right to stay and refuses to back down. The duo also chase Â£5,000 for an unpaid parking fine, which comes as a shock to the debtor, who has clearly hit hard times.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Ben face a challenging eviction in London, where a vulnerable tenant in rent arrears has barricaded her front door, and the agents receive a tirade of abuse. Meanwhile, Brian and Del chase a debt of nearly Â£2,000 in Surrey, before trying to recover almost Â£3,000 in Northamptonshire. Finally, Steve and Ru head to Portsmouth to evict a young family for a landlord who wants her property back.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement agents Paul Bohill and Steve Pinner are in Birmingham on a potentially dangerous, full-scale eviction of a large traveller community. Meanwhile, in London's diamond district Brian O'Shaughnessy and Delroy Anglin are chasing nearly Â£6,000 owed by a jeweller to an unhappy customer, although a solicitor tries to convince Delroy his client owes nothing.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court Enforcement Agents Paul and Steve are sent to north London to evict a young Romanian mother who owes Â£6,000 in unpaid rent, but when they arrive, they discover a family home that is not quite what it appears to be. Meanwhile, in Kent, Brian and Alan turn up the pressure on a debtor who has admitted to owing more than Â£7,000, but claims he cannot afford to pay up - despite having a garage full of expensive tools. Later, in south-west London, Brian accompanies Delroy as he tries to recover a Â£400,000 debt from a residential property, and when the pair trace the defaulter to an upmarket cafe, the man in charge lies about his identity.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A young mum locked out of her home by the young couple she allowed to stay there for free while the wife had a baby. Plus, an east London landlord who found half a dozen strangers living in his house, after his original tenant had run off and sublet the property. Meanwhile, Harrow Council raid a small two-bedroom flat in unsafe conditions.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The efforts of north London landlord Franc to evict a tenant withholding rent payments on a property whose boiler mysteriously stopped working whenever the rent was due, and a tenant with asthma who has been forced to sleep on his sofa for two years in a damp flat. Plus, a landlord owed Â£7,000 in rent discovers her nightmare is not over when she evicts the tenant and gains access to her property.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A Yorkshire landlord has to call in eviction specialists to remove tenants from the house he inherited from his grandparents. Music entrepreneur Donovan has not received rent from one of his tenants for seven months, so the case ends up in court.(n)

programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. programme.title: Con Air (1997)
programme.desc: Honourably discharged Army Ranger sergeant Cameron Poe hitches a ride home on a maximum-security flight conveying some of America's deadliest criminals. When they hijack the plane in mid-flight, led by criminal mastermind Cyrus the Virus, Poe is forced to intervene. Meanwhile, US marshal Vince Larkin has an ongoing battle on the ground to prevent the plane from being blown to pieces. Director Simon West's blockbusting action thriller, starring Nicolas Cage, John Cusack, John Malkovich, Steve Buscemi, Ving Rhames and Colm Meaney.(n)

programme.desc: A man is found stabbed to death in an alleyway, and the detectives discover he shined shoes for a living. They begin by looking for his most recent customers, and learn the victim was a Vietnam war veteran, shedding light on possible motives and suspects.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Green and Cassady investigate the death of corporate attorney Charles Dillon, whose body was found in a hotel room, and discover the company he was working for was in the process of being indicted. Their inquiry leads them to the female CEO and her relationship with a prostitute. Crime drama, starring Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A man's death offers no immediate explanation, until the autopsy points Green and Cassady in the direction of illegally harvested donor bones, which the dead man had received in a transplant. However, McCoy and Rubirosa struggle to bring the case to prosecution when it becomes clear they can only get the necessary evidence by testing another transplant recipient.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: McCoy and Southerlyn battle the lawyers over the fatal shooting of an uninvited nightclub guest who was seen confronting popular rap star Darry 'G-Trane' moments before the murder. Award-winning police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Elisabeth Rohm.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The partially dissolved body of a submariner is discovered in a tub of acid at a Navy base - but Gibbs discovers no crew members have been reported missing and deduces an imposter has assumed the dead man's identity. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Sasha Alexander.(n)

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Law & Order

Crime drama, starring Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The ex-wife of a respected former senator is found murdered in her home, and Green and Cassady's inquiries lead them to a dysfunctional family with a number of strange secrets. programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Columbo: Try and Catch Me (1977)
programme.desc: A grieving crime writer blames her niece's husband for her death and concocts a plan to take revenge, luring him to her walk-in vault, before locking him in and leaving him to die. She then puts her knowledge of the criminal mind to good use by covering her tracks, and although Columbo strongly suspects foul play, he finds clues are thin on the ground. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk and Ruth Gordon.(n)

programme.desc: A compulsive gambler with a mountain of debts comes up with a plan to make some easy money. He plants a bomb under his wealthy uncle's car in the hope of inheriting a large legacy - but a hit-and-run driver jeopardises the entire scheme and Columbo is soon sniffing around for clues. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Tyne Daly, Greg Evigan and Don S Davis.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Ransom for a Dead Man (1971)
programme.desc: A devious lawyer hatches the perfect murder plot - she kills her husband, tells the police he has been kidnapped and leaves the body at the ransom drop, making it look like the criminals who abducted him finished him off. However, the crumpled cop sees through her ruse and begins picking apart her story in his inimitable manner. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Lee Grant and Patricia Mattick.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Prescription Murder (1968)
programme.desc: A psychiatrist having an affair with a patient murders his wife with his lover's help. However, while he establishes the perfect alibi he soon finds himself pursued by Columbo, whose meandering investigative approach conceals an uncanny gift for detection. First episode of the detective series, starring Peter Falk and Gene Barry.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Caution - Murder Can Be Hazardous to Your Health (1991)
programme.desc: The host of a TV show is threatened by a rival, who has discovered evidence of his murky past. The presenter does away with his blackmailer using poisoned cigarettes, and seems to have pulled off the perfect crime - but a certain shabby sleuth is soon on the case and finds a few things that fail to add up. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk and George Hamilton.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: A Friend in Deed (1974)
programme.desc: A Californian police commissioner helps a rich, influential friend to conceal his complicity in murdering his own wife - but reckons without detective Columbo's timely intervention and his dogged determination to bring the truth to light. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Richard Kiley and Rosemary Murphy.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A billionaire's stepson drops dead on a dance floor, the apparent victim of a deliberately administered drug overdose, and suspicion initially falls on his mother, a former stripper and model - but she too is later killed. Drama, starring Chris Noth, with Kristy Swanson and Arrested Development's David Cross.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team is called in when a psychologist is found murdered outside a convenience store and all the evidence points to one of her patients - a disturbed man who refuses to take his medication. However, the discovery of a second, surviving victim behind the shop blows the lid off its theory.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A policeman's past comes back to haunt him when his wife alleges he raped her. As inquiries into the case deepen, a history of violence is exposed. Cragen is shocked by Jeffries' reaction when she is unceremoniously demoted for inappropriate conduct. Hard-hitting drama, guest starring Nestor Serrano and Amy Carlson, with Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Detectives investigate the sexual assault and suspicious death of an elderly woman, and question two teenagers who will only admit to breaking into the victim's apartment - until they confess somebody else was in there at the same time. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: Call the Cleaners
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of extreme cleaners. In London, sisters Yvonne and Angela tackle the dirtiest flat they have ever encountered in nearly 20 years on the job, while Maxine and her daughter Jasmine deal with a hoarder who has 20 years' worth of possessions cluttering his home. In Somerset, Shaun and his team specialise in full house clearances and hey are sorting out a flat for a landlord, but the resident appears not to have thrown anything away in years and rotting household rubbish almost fills the rooms.(n)

programme.title: Call the Cleaners
programme.desc: Shaun and his team are called out by a landlady who found her property in a very bad state, with mess over the floors, graffiti on the walls and doors pulled off their hinges. Maxine and her daughter Jasmine encounter a mountain of cigarette butts while cleaning the house of a man whose life fell apart following the breakup of his marriage, while sisters Yvonne and Angela tackle a bedsit with an infestation of German cockroaches.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Swansea to meet 15-year-old Jack, who suffers from a rare form of muscular dystrophy that has led to a lifetime of painful operations. His mother Helen and older brother Daniel do all they can to help but as he grows it has become impossible to get him upstairs and now he has to sleep in the living room, leaving the rest of the family with nowhere to socialise. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team turns up to transform their three-bedroom semi, backed up by an army of local tradespeople, with designer Gabrielle Blackman on hand to ensure they don't stray too far from her vision.(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner.(n)

The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner.(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts a celebrity version of the classic game show, in which he invites well-known faces and their clans to win Â£30,000 for charity. Radio 1 DJ Chris Moyles plays for the Robert Ogden Centre and the Iain Rennie Hospice at Home, while presenter Fearne Cotton aims to win big for Macmillan Cancer Support.(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts another celebrity edition of the classic game show, in which he invites well-known faces to team up with their relatives in a bid to win a prize of up to Â£30,000 for charity. Actor Nicola Stapleton joins her clan to play for Cancer Research, while comedian Johnny Vegas and his family aim to win big for the Willowbrook Hospice.(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Vernon Kay invites another celebrity pair to team up with their relatives in a bid to win a prize of up to Â£30,000 for charity. Radio 1 DJ Sara Cox and her brood play for Macmillan Cancer Support and Magic Breakfast, while singer Jimmy Osmond brings his family along to raise money for the LDS Humanitarian Relief Fund.(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Vernon Kay invites another pair of famous faces to team up with their relatives and play the classic game show, in a bid to win a prize of up to Â£30,000 for charity. Chef Jean-Christophe Novelli and his brood aim to win for SOS Children's Village and Chase Children's Hospice, Guildford, while antiques expert David Dickinson brings his family along to raise funds for St Ann's Hospice, Cheshire.(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Vernon Kay invites another pair of well-known faces to play the classic game show in a bid to win a prize of up to Â£30,000 for charity. Former Blue singer Lee Ryan brings his clan along to raise funds for the Elton John Aids Foundation, while model Melinda Messenger's brood aim to win big for ActionAid.(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Vernon Kay invites another pair of famous faces to play the classic game show in a bid to win up to Â£30,000 for charity. Sports presenter Gabby Logan and her clan aim to earn cash for Chase Children's Hospice in Guildford, while former England goalkeeper David Seaman brings his brood along to raise money for the Bobby Moore Fund for Cancer Research.(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Vernon Kay invites another pair of famous faces to play the classic game show in a bid to win up to Â£30,000 for charity. Olympian Kelly Holmes and her clan aim to earn cash for Breast Cancer Haven, while cricketer and former King of the Jungle Phil Tufnell brings his brood along to raise money for the Samantha Dixon Brain Tumour Trust.(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Vernon Kay invites cast members from two soap families to play the classic game show in a bid to win up to Â£30,000 for a charity of their choice. Coronation Street's Bruce Jones (Les Battersby-Brown) teams up with screen wife Wendi Peters (Cilla), Andrew Whyment (lodger Kirk Sutherland), Samia Smith (Kirk's sister Maria) and Jayne Tunnicliffe (family friend Yana Lumb) to play for the Leukaemia Research Fund and the Leeds Upper GI Fund. Meanwhile, Emmerdale star Tony Audenshaw (Bob Hope) brings along his on-screen spouse Deena Payne (Viv), Verity Rushworth (stepdaughter Donna Windsor-Dingle), Alex Carter (son Jamie) and Adele Silva (Kelly Windsor), aiming to win big for the Variety Club of Great Britain and the Five Stars Scanner Appeal.(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Birds of a Feather actress Pauline Quirke takes part in the game show with broadcaster Jo Whiley. The pair team up with their families to guess the answers to surveys of 100 people in a bid to reach the final round, where they can win up to Â£30,000 for a charity of their choice. Vernon Kay hosts.(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as former Tory MP Edwina Currie, Countdown maths whizz Rachel Riley, Boyzone's Keith Duffy and Emmerdale's Mark Charnock (Marlon Dingle) pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for their chosen charities. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught.(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as comedian Jimmy Carr, Coronation Street's Jack P Shepherd (David Platt), retired rugby union player Matt Dawson and former Big Brother star Sally Bercow pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught.(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: TV presenter Dan Lobb, musical theatre star Jodie Prenger, wildlife expert Chris Packham and Coronation Street actress Jennie McAlpine (Fiz Stape) compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by pitting their general knowledge against one of the show's resident trivia experts. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Presented by Bradley Walsh.(n)
